[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 seconds\nOutput:\ndiff --git a/README.md b/README.md\nindex 3067972..6b8417e 100644\n--- a/README.md\n+++ b/README.md\n@@ -1,82 +1,84 @@\n # The Forever Treasure Companion\n \n Production-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\n \n ## Current phase\n \n Phase two expands the production foundation into the complete Player Companion shell: multi-state Journal, Voyage Chart, Treasure Altar, Side-Quest Ledger, event-derived Ship’s Log, sealed Finale, persistent objective, companion navigation, cross-device unseen state, preferences, and fullscreen-friendly operation. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\n \n+The Phase 3 feature branch adds an expanded Game Master Command Center: 12 URL-backed workspaces, truthful presence/synchronization, sanitized Player View, nonmutating projection, staging, idempotent commands, stale-tab protection, recovery, audit/diagnostics, keyboard controls, and tablet/emergency layouts. It remains unintegrated until the parallel Phase 2 reconciliation pass.\n+\n ## Stack\n \n - Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\n - Prisma 6.19 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\n - Framer Motion with pinned compatible motion packages\n - bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\n - Vitest and Playwright\n \n ## Reproducible setup\n \n Requirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\n \n ### Fastest Windows demo\n \n After cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\n \n ```powershell\n npm run dev:full\n ```\n \n The launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, seeds generic disposable data, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\n \n - Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n - Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\n - GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n - GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\n \n These values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\n \n ### Portable manual setup\n \n ```bash\n git clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\n cd treasurehuntSoT\n npm ci\n cp .env.example .env\n npm run db:generate\n npm run db:migrate\n npm run db:seed\n npm run dev\n ```\n \n Set unique local values in `.env`; the seed’s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\n \n ## Commands\n \n | Command                           | Purpose                                                                |\n | --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n | `npm run dev:full`                | Idempotent setup, migration, seed, health check, and background server |\n | `npm run dev:stop`                | Safely stop only the recorded development process                      |\n | `npm run validate`                | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof          |\n | `npm run dev`                     | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                        |\n | `npm run build`                   | Production build                                                       |\n | `npm start`                       | Start production build                                                 |\n-| `npm test`                        | Six deterministic unit tests                                           |\n-| `npm run test:e2e`                | Five Chromium/WebKit checks plus one Chromium mutation workflow        |\n+| `npm test`                        | Complete Phase 2/3 domain, visibility, presence, and ceremony tests    |\n+| `npm run test:e2e`                | Cross-browser player and Command Center acceptance matrix              |\n | `npm run db:migrate`              | Apply local SQLite migrations                                          |\n | `npm run db:seed`                 | Load replaceable development content                                   |\n | `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage` | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset                |\n | `npm run db:generate:mysql`       | Generate production MySQL client                                       |\n \n Full setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md).\n \n ## Repository workflow\n \n `main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\n \n ## Important limitations\n \n The full story, final art/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural; no external copyrighted assets are present. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\n \n ## Next steps\n \n Add CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\ndiff --git a/docs/action-risk-model.md b/docs/action-risk-model.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..bc6a7f2\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/action-risk-model.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@\n+# Action risk model\n+\n+- Low: navigation, refresh, preview, reconciliation request.\n+- Medium: preparation and staging; clear acknowledgement, no typed phrase.\n+- High: player-visible release, solve/complete, pause/resume, award, reveal, and undo; server projection and confirmation required.\n+- Critical: force progression, multi-event revert, reset, finale unlock; not exposed in Phase 3.\n+\n+Confirmation shows command, event, current/projected sequence, affected systems, ceremony, undo availability, and unmet prerequisites. A prerequisite failure disables commit and is announced without relying on color.\ndiff --git a/docs/admin-command-pipeline.md b/docs/admin-command-pipeline.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..165e31c\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/admin-command-pipeline.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@\n+# Administrative command pipeline\n+\n+```mermaid\n+flowchart LR\n+  A[Authenticated GM] --> B[CSRF + schema validation]\n+  B --> C[Authoritative state]\n+  C --> D[Expected sequence]\n+  D --> E[Idempotency record]\n+  E --> F[Domain transaction]\n+  F --> G[(State + event + snapshot + audit)]\n+  G --> H[Commit]\n+  H --> I[SSE publication]\n+  I --> J[Persistence and delivery reported separately]\n+```\n+\n+The client supplies intent, not current state. `expectedSequence` protects stale tabs; `(campaignId,idempotencyKey)` is unique; domain uniqueness protects repeat awards and releases. Completed duplicates replay their stored result. Failures retain correlation and sanitized code.\n+\n+Preview follows validation → projected clone → player visibility → consequence summary. It creates no idempotency record, sequence, audit/event row, or SSE publication.\ndiff --git a/docs/architecture.md b/docs/architecture.md\nindex 9d52c9d..c15bf74 100644\n--- a/docs/architecture.md\n+++ b/docs/architecture.md\n@@ -1,20 +1,22 @@\n # Architecture\n \n ## Phase 2 companion projection\n \n The player reads one server-filtered `PublicSnapshot` containing chapters, released hints/annotations, visible map locations/routes, safe artifact states, visible optional mysteries, event-derived log entries, generic finale state, and per-section unseen counts. One SSE connection transports ordered sanitized events; section navigation is client-side and starts no independent polling.\n \n New normalized records are `MapRoute` and `ViewedContent`. Existing content models gained release, relationship, placement, and safe-label fields rather than duplicating derived display tables. `AudioPreference` now carries the cross-device preference projection; immediate UI choices may also be cached locally for offline startup.\n \n ```mermaid\n flowchart LR\n   P[Player journal] -->|snapshot + access cookie| N[Next.js server]\n   G[Quartermaster] -->|session + CSRF| N\n   N -->|Prisma transaction| D[(SQLite dev / MySQL prod)]\n   N -->|ordered SSE + replay| P\n   N --> A[Audit and save-state log]\n ```\n \n Server Components enforce initial access. Route handlers own authentication, validation, snapshots, and SSE. `src/server/progression.ts` is the transaction boundary; `src/domain/story.ts` owns state rules. The UI receives only a public projection, never database rows. In-memory publish accelerates same-process delivery while database replay by sequence remains authoritative after reconnect/restart.\n \n Major dependencies are deliberately limited: Prisma for normalized persistence and transactions, bcryptjs for portable hashes, Zod for payload validation, Framer Motion for accessible choreography, Pino for structured redacted logs, Vitest/Playwright for validation.\n+\n+Phase 3 adds an administrative intent layer. `/api/gm/preview` projects without writes, `/api/gm/staging` persists unreleased intent, and `/api/gm/commands` enforces expected sequence and idempotency. `PlayerPresence` supplies expiring evidence. See [admin command pipeline](admin-command-pipeline.md) and [player presence](player-presence-and-sync.md).\ndiff --git a/docs/audit-and-event-history.md b/docs/audit-and-event-history.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..5f055fa\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/audit-and-event-history.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@\n+# Audit and event history\n+\n+Progress events are the ordered player-facing/domain history; audits are administrative history. Successful commands record actor, command, time, target, event/sequence where applicable, correlation, outcome, and optional reason. Failures record a sanitized code. Credentials are never metadata.\n+\n+Event and Audit workspaces load the latest 80 records and constrain long scroll regions. Server cursor pagination is the next step beyond this development scale.\ndiff --git a/docs/codex-handoff.md b/docs/codex-handoff.md\nindex 90df059..16f2653 100644\n--- a/docs/codex-handoff.md\n+++ b/docs/codex-handoff.md\n@@ -1,71 +1,16 @@\n # Codex handoff\n \n-- Canonical repository: `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`, branch `main`\n-- Phase completed: Phase 2 — complete Player Companion shell\n-- Repository visibility: **public** as verified 2026-07-16; only fictional development content is permitted\n-- Latest validated implementation commit: `0f510a3f218a464259f19efa4f6b8991a8546f4f`\n-- Updated: 2026-07-16\n-\n-## Player surfaces\n-\n-- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n-- Game Master: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n-- Sections: `journal`, `chart`, `treasures`, `quests`, `log`, `finale`\n-- Navigation: `?section=<id>` with pushState/popstate restoration, shared desktop/mobile state, and one SSE connection\n-- Persistent current objective, compact preferences, player-initiated fullscreen, artifact inspection, chapter index, accessible map list, and event-derived log are implemented\n-\n-## Architecture\n-\n-- Next.js App Router, Prisma SQLite/MySQL, database-backed sessions, ordered domain events/SSE, central ceremony queue\n-- Public snapshots are server allowlist projections; raw event payloads never reach the player stream\n-- New models: `MapRoute`, `ViewedContent`\n-- Extended models: campaign finale shell; chapter safe teasers/cross-links; artifact release/assembly state; map safe labels/reveal metadata; optional quest rewards/links; journal annotations; companion preferences\n-- New event families: route reveal, artifact silhouette/connection, quest completion, annotations/log entries, finale tease/requirements\n-- Ship's Log is derived by `src/domain/ships-log.ts`, not stored as duplicate display history\n-- Unseen state is cross-device per player access identity; ceremony playback remains device-local\n-\n-## Development presets\n-\n-Run `npm run db:preset -- <preset>` to reset the development campaign. Supported values:\n-\n-- `awaiting-first-release` (default seed)\n-- `active-chapter`\n-- `mid-voyage`\n-- `artifact-award-ready`\n-- `side-quest-active`\n-- `nearly-complete-shell`\n-- `all-empty`\n-- `long-log`\n-- `mobile-stress-test`\n-\n-Presets are CLI-only, safe to repeat, development-content-only, and not exposed to players or production UI.\n-\n-## Commands\n-\n-- Start everything: `npm run dev:full`\n-- Stop: `npm run dev:stop`\n-- Complete validation: `npm run validate`\n-- SQLite generate/migrate/seed: `npm run db:generate && npm run db:migrate && npm run db:seed`\n-- MySQL companion migration: apply `db:migrate:mysql:init` then `db:migrate:mysql:companion`\n-\n-Local development defaults come from `.env.example`: player phrase `development-moonwake`; GM `kato` / `development-captain-only`. Never use them outside an isolated local development database.\n-\n-## Validation state\n-\n-- Strict TypeScript: passing\n-- Unit tests: 13 passing across 5 files\n-- ESLint: passing\n-- Production build: passing\n-- SQLite migrations: clean application of init + companion migration\n-- `mid-voyage` fixture integrity: 12 contiguous events, 12 snapshots, 12 audits\n-- In-app browser: all six sections, deep-link history, artifact dialog, sealed finale, 1440/1920 desktop and 390 mobile states inspected with zero horizontal overflow and no console warnings\n-- Chromium/WebKit automated acceptance and clean-clone validation are part of the final task gate; consult the ending task report for exact counts/artifacts\n-\n-## Known limitations and next phase\n-\n-- Real story, riddles, locations, photographs, personal memories, romantic messages, production tokens, and finale content remain blocked until the repository is private.\n-- Finale states beyond `REQUIREMENTS_PARTIAL` and real assembly are intentionally unavailable.\n-- SSE fan-out is process-local; add Redis/equivalent before multi-instance deployment.\n-- Initial log projection is capped at 250 entries; add cursor pagination for larger production histories.\n-- Exercise the parallel MySQL migration against MySQL 8 in CI.\n-- Recommended Phase 3: make the repository private, establish the Creative Bible/content pipeline, then author final story through versioned private content without changing the shell architecture.\n+- Canonical repository: `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`\n+- Phase 3 branch: `feature/game-master-command-center`; original base `70bb654b78a84df15dba8d0f9ce5b3fd5782181d`\n+- Integration base: Phase 2 merged `origin/main` at `ede3764`; reconciled by a normal merge, preserving both histories\n+- Status: expanded Command Center plus complete Phase 2 Player Companion; not integrated back into `main`\n+- Command Center routes: `/quartermaster` plus `/chapters`, `/hints`, `/voyage`, `/artifacts`, `/quests`, `/journal`, `/events`, `/player-view`, `/recovery`, `/audit`, `/diagnostics`\n+- Authentication: GM bcrypt/database session and CSRF; player sessions cannot call admin APIs\n+- Commands: prepare/release/solve/complete chapter, prepare/release hint, reveal map, award artifact, discover/advance quest, release journal entry, pause/resume, undo, reconciliation\n+- Migration: Phase 2 `20260716223000_player_companion_shell`, then Phase 3 `20260716233000_game_master_command_center`; connector-parity MySQL SQL exists\n+- Development data: Phase 2 multi-state presets plus Phase 3 prepared-action and audit fixtures\n+- Startup: `npm run dev:full`; validation: `npm run validate`; stop: `npm run dev:stop`\n+- Known limitations: no background scheduler, multi-event rollback, automated presence cleanup, Redis fan-out, real finale/story/private media, or deployment\n+- Latest fully validated implementation commit: `8174f28` (16 unit, 9 executed browser, build, and two restart proofs)\n+- Draft pull request: `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/1`\n+- Recommended next task: controlled integration review and production-readiness work; do not merge the PR without the post-merge checklist\ndiff --git a/docs/command-center-information-architecture.md b/docs/command-center-information-architecture.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..85af0d5\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/command-center-information-architecture.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@\n+# Command Center information architecture\n+\n+| Route                        | Workspace     | Primary task                           |\n+| ---------------------------- | ------------- | -------------------------------------- |\n+| `/quartermaster`             | Command Deck  | status truth and immediate operations  |\n+| `/quartermaster/chapters`    | Chapters      | state-machine progression              |\n+| `/quartermaster/hints`       | Hints         | ordered preparation and release        |\n+| `/quartermaster/voyage`      | Voyage        | location visibility and chart plotting |\n+| `/quartermaster/artifacts`   | Artifacts     | award state and ceremony preview       |\n+| `/quartermaster/quests`      | Side Quests   | discovery and advancement              |\n+| `/quartermaster/journal`     | Journal       | plain-text narrative dispatches        |\n+| `/quartermaster/events`      | Event Staging | prepared actions and immutable events  |\n+| `/quartermaster/player-view` | Player View   | sanitized released-state mirror        |\n+| `/quartermaster/recovery`    | Recovery      | reversal and emergency controls        |\n+| `/quartermaster/audit`       | Audit         | actor/outcome/correlation history      |\n+| `/quartermaster/diagnostics` | Diagnostics   | presence, sequence lag, capabilities   |\n+\n+All routes use the authenticated shell, browser history, and server truth. Player navigation never links to them. Below 900 px the rail becomes an emergency dock with Pause/Resume, Player View, Next Hint, and Undo. `Ctrl/Cmd+K` opens the command palette, `Escape` closes overlays, and `P` opens pause/resume confirmation.\ndiff --git a/docs/game-master-controls.md b/docs/game-master-controls.md\nindex fcc4d68..d2a0f83 100644\n--- a/docs/game-master-controls.md\n+++ b/docs/game-master-controls.md\n@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@\n # Game Master controls\n \n-## Phase 2 controls\n+The Phase 3 Command Center uses select → server projection → consequence confirmation → atomic command → persistence receipt → separate delivery observation. It exposes Command Deck, Chapters, Hints, Voyage, Artifacts, Side Quests, Journal, Event Staging, Player View, Recovery, Audit, and Diagnostics.\n \n-Authenticated progression supports map/route reveal, artifact award/silhouette/connection, side-quest discover/update/complete, journal annotation, player-facing log entry, finale tease, and generic finale requirement update. Each call requires session authentication, CSRF, explicit confirmation, a transaction, a domain event, a campaign snapshot, and an audit row. Development presets are CLI-only and never appear in the player or production UI.\n+No control sets arbitrary state. Chapter commands follow the seven-state machine, hints remain ordered, awards are unique, pause blocks progression, and undo emits a compensating event. Preview is watermarked and nonmutating. Critical finale/reset controls are absent. See [information architecture](command-center-information-architecture.md), [risk model](action-risk-model.md), and [recovery](recovery-and-reversal.md).\n \n-Prepare, release, solve, artifact award, map reveal, pause, resume, and undo all use select → impact preview → confirmation → atomic execution → event receipt. The release transaction activates the chapter and reveals its intentionally imprecise map marker. Undo restores the latest save-state and emits `STATE_REVERTED` so players reconcile. Preview Reveal and Replay Reveal Locally are represented by the safe player replay control in this slice; a dedicated isolated GM preview timeline is future work.\n+The Phase 2 Player Companion consumes the same sanitized snapshot and immutable event stream. Its expanded voyage chart, relic altar, side-quest ledger, journal, Ship’s Log, and finale shell remain player-owned surfaces; Command Center changes must preserve their visibility filtering.\ndiff --git a/docs/known-limitations.md b/docs/known-limitations.md\nindex 372c4a2..9cbb6c6 100644\n--- a/docs/known-limitations.md\n+++ b/docs/known-limitations.md\n@@ -1,17 +1,20 @@\n # Known limitations\n \n ## Phase 2 boundaries\n \n - The repository is public; final story and surprise content is intentionally blocked.\n - Finale unlock/assembly is structural only and cannot enter `READY` or later states through Phase 2 controls.\n - SSE fan-out remains process-local and needs Redis or equivalent before multi-instance production deployment.\n - Initial Ship's Log projection is capped at 250 entries; cursor pagination is future scale work.\n - Production MySQL migration must be exercised against an actual MySQL 8 integration environment.\n - Final art/audio, real content authoring, and production deployment belong to later phases.\n \n - SSE fan-out is process-local; database replay is durable, but multi-instance production needs Redis/pub-sub.\n-- Dedicated GM-only preview timeline, explicit replay targeting, and richer player presence are not yet implemented.\n+- The Phase 3 preview is a sanitized state projection rather than a pixel-identical embedded Player Companion ceremony timeline; explicit replay targeting is not implemented.\n - The full mutation workflow runs once in Chromium to avoid cross-project database contention; privacy, route protection, accessibility, and mobile behavior also run in WebKit.\n - Windows LAN access is opt-in and may still require a firewall rule or network-policy change; localhost is the safe default.\n - SQLite and MySQL schemas are intentionally parallel; schema edits must update both and add SQL to the connector-specific migration histories until a generation workflow is added.\n - Final story content, private media, polished licensed audio, production monitoring, and deployment remain out of scope.\n+- Scheduling stores a future timestamp and stale-state evidence but has no background execution worker; scheduled actions require Game Master review and release.\n+- Recovery exposes only the latest safe single-step reversal. Full dependency-graph multi-event rollback is intentionally unavailable.\n+- Presence is durable per device but cleanup is documented rather than automated; production should expire records older than 30 days.\ndiff --git a/docs/parallel-development-phase-3.md b/docs/parallel-development-phase-3.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..e7805e2\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/parallel-development-phase-3.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@\n+# Phase 3 parallel-development record\n+\n+- Base and starting commit: `70bb654b78a84df15dba8d0f9ce5b3fd5782181d`\n+- Branch: `feature/game-master-command-center`\n+- Base branch: `origin/main`\n+- Worktree: isolated from the dirty Phase 2 checkout\n+- Reconciliation: Phase 2 landed on `origin/main` at `ede3764`; that updated main was merged into this feature branch after the initial Phase 3 validation. Phase 2 player visibility/rendering and seed presets were retained, with Phase 3 administrative relations, presence lifecycle, CSS, and fixtures applied additively.\n+\n+## Shared contract review\n+\n+Existing behavior uses `src/domain/story.ts` for seven chapter states and ordered player events, `src/lib/snapshot.ts` for the player-safe projection, and `src/server/progression.ts` as the Phase 1 transaction boundary. Phase 3 needs administrative commands, staging, preview, idempotency, presence, and audit correlation. Phase 2 is likely to touch `story.ts`, both Prisma schemas, seed data, the player component, and the snapshot.\n+\n+Compatibility strategy: administrative schemas live in `src/domain/admin.ts`; new tables and audit fields are additive; existing fields and endpoints remain; presence is additive to legacy `lastSeenAt`; the public snapshot shape is unchanged; player event additions use optional version-1 payloads. Preview clones the sanitized snapshot and never writes.\n+\n+Shared files modified: `src/domain/story.ts`, `src/server/progression.ts`, `src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx`, both Prisma schemas, `prisma/seed.ts`, and appended Command Center CSS. New events are `HINT_PREPARED`, `MAP_REVEAL_PREPARED`, `ARTIFACT_AWARD_PREPARED`, `SIDE_QUEST_UPDATE_PREPARED`, `PLAYER_RECONCILIATION_REQUESTED`, and `NARRATIVE_MESSAGE_RELEASED`.\n+\n+New APIs: `POST /api/gm/commands`, `POST /api/gm/preview`, `POST /api/gm/staging`, and `POST /api/player/[campaignSlug]/presence`. Admin routes require a GM session; writes also require CSRF. Commands validate expected sequence and idempotency key.\n+\n+Resolved conflicts were Phase 2 snapshot/event additions, schema relations on `PlayerAccess`, seed fixtures, the player lifecycle, global styles, Game Master UI, verification rules, and handoff docs. Phase 2 visibility and Player Companion stayed authoritative; Phase 3 Command Center stayed authoritative. Post-merge validation regenerates both clients, migrates clean SQLite, and runs unit, E2E, accessibility, build, two-context SSE, preview-nonmutation, stale-command, and leakage checks.\ndiff --git a/docs/phase-2-phase-3-integration.md b/docs/phase-2-phase-3-integration.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..566e786\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/phase-2-phase-3-integration.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@\n+# Phase 2 and Phase 3 integration plan\n+\n+```mermaid\n+flowchart TD\n+  A[Integrate Phase 2 player contracts] --> B[Resolve event union additively]\n+  B --> C[Combine Prisma models and migrations]\n+  C --> D[Retain Phase 2 visibility projection]\n+  D --> E[Adapt presence to final player routes]\n+  E --> F[Clean migration + two-browser suite]\n+```\n+\n+This branch began on pre-Phase-2 `main` at `70bb654` and now includes Phase 2 `main` through `ede3764` via a normal merge commit. Phase 2 remains authoritative for player rendering/visibility; Phase 3 remains authoritative for administrative intent, presence, execution, staging, and audit correlation.\n+\n+Migration order: Phase 1 init, Phase 2 additive migration, then Phase 3 command-center migration. Reconciliation tests cover older snapshots, hidden content, hint/artifact/map/quest delivery, route presence, acknowledgement, SSE reconnect, preview nonmutation, stale commands, and reversal.\ndiff --git a/docs/player-presence-and-sync.md b/docs/player-presence-and-sync.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..6b2bcd4\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/player-presence-and-sync.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@\n+# Player presence and synchronization\n+\n+```mermaid\n+sequenceDiagram\n+  participant P as Player browser\n+  participant S as Server\n+  participant D as Database\n+  participant G as Command Center\n+  P->>S: heartbeat: device, route, visibility, acknowledged sequence\n+  S->>D: upsert PlayerPresence\n+  G->>S: GET status\n+  S->>D: load evidence and campaign sequence\n+  S-->>G: state, devices, last seen, lag\n+```\n+\n+Connected means a non-disconnected heartbeat within 45 seconds. Recently lost means evidence within 120 seconds. Older evidence is stale; no evidence is unknown. A connected transport is never labeled “viewed.” Synchronized requires an active device whose acknowledged sequence equals the campaign sequence. Future production maintenance should expire records older than 30 days.\ndiff --git a/docs/recovery-and-reversal.md b/docs/recovery-and-reversal.md\nnew file mode 100644\nindex 0000000..55ba05a\n--- /dev/null\n+++ b/docs/recovery-and-reversal.md\n@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@\n+# Recovery and reversal\n+\n+```mermaid\n+flowchart TD\n+  A[Select latest save point] --> B{Later dependent event?}\n+  B -->|yes| C[Block and explain dependency]\n+  B -->|no| D[Restore state in transaction]\n+  D --> E[Append STATE_REVERTED]\n+  E --> F[Publish reconciliation]\n+  F --> G[Player fetches snapshot]\n+```\n+\n+Phase 3 surfaces save points, labels only the latest as directly reversible, and preserves history through a compensating event. It never deletes the reversed event. Multi-event rollback and arbitrary restoration remain unavailable. Pause preserves released content; resume requires stale staged actions to be previewed again.\ndiff --git a/docs/security.md b/docs/security.md\nindex d9fab04..a145f7d 100644\n--- a/docs/security.md\n+++ b/docs/security.md\n@@ -1,13 +1,15 @@\n # Security\n \n ## Companion release boundary\n \n Serialization is allowlist-based. Locked chapters omit narrative/objective/clues; unreleased hints and annotations are absent; rumored map locations omit coordinates/internal regions; unknown artifacts omit names/descriptions; hidden quests are absent and rumors expose only safe teasers; hidden finale details are never modeled in public content. SSE payloads pass through the same explicit allowlist and never stream raw stored payloads. Player routes require a campaign-bound, unexpired access identity and expose no mutation endpoint.\n \n The repository is public as of 2026-07-16. Real story, surprise, romantic, location, photographic, token, and finale content is prohibited until visibility is verified private.\n \n Player access uses a bcrypt-verified campaign code and strict HTTP-only cookie. GM login uses bcrypt, database sessions, secure/strict cookies, a per-session CSRF token, generic failures, and a five-failure/15-minute fingerprint limit. Zod validates mutation input. Admin actions are audited. Logs redact passwords, access codes, cookies, and authorization headers. Player snapshots omit all chapter content until the chapter is released; tests and manual DOM/network inspection verify the gate contains no clue text.\n \n Production must set a random 32+ byte session secret, unique GM password and player code, HTTPS, reverse-proxy rate controls, database least privilege, and private repository visibility before real surprise content is committed.\n \n The repository was still treated as public during the automated-demo milestone. Only generic development clues, locations, messages, and credentials are committed; validation performs a deliberate diff/secret review before publication. LAN development is an explicit opt-in because binding to `0.0.0.0` exposes the disposable demo surface to peers allowed by the host firewall.\n+\n+Phase 3 admin commands require GM session, CSRF, schema validation, expected sequence, and idempotency key. The player cookie cannot call admin APIs. Preview uses the sanitized public projection and performs no writes. Narrative content renders as React text, not HTML. Development capability exposure is server-gated by `NODE_ENV`; finale/reset commands are not implemented. Correlation/audit metadata excludes secrets.\ndiff --git a/docs/story-state-machine.md b/docs/story-state-machine.md\nindex 8076aab..5e6b5bc 100644\n--- a/docs/story-state-machine.md\n+++ b/docs/story-state-machine.md\n@@ -1,21 +1,23 @@\n # Story state machine\n \n ## Companion sub-state\n \n Chapter transitions remain authoritative. Map, route, artifact, side-quest, and finale state changes are separate domain events projected into the same campaign snapshot. They never bypass chapter visibility. New event types include route reveal, artifact silhouette/connection, quest completion, annotation/log arrival, and finale tease/requirement changes. Undo captures every mutable companion subsystem before applying a progression action.\n \n ```mermaid\n stateDiagram-v2\n   LOCKED --> TEASER\n   LOCKED --> READY\n   TEASER --> READY\n   READY --> REVEALING\n   REVEALING --> ACTIVE\n   ACTIVE --> SOLVED\n   SOLVED --> COMPLETE\n   READY --> LOCKED: undo\n   ACTIVE --> READY: undo\n   SOLVED --> ACTIVE: undo\n ```\n \n The schema stores state and ordered immutable events rather than a chapter number alone. The vertical slice releases READY directly to durable ACTIVE in one transaction so animation failure never blocks truth; REVEALING is a client ceremony phase and remains available for future server acknowledgements. Every mutation saves a restore point, increments a campaign sequence, creates an event/snapshot/audit record, then publishes after commit. Event IDs plus device ceremony acknowledgements provide idempotency.\n+\n+Administrative clients cannot set state directly. They submit named commands with an expected sequence. Pause blocks progression except resume, undo, and diagnostics. Ordered hints, unique awards, side-quest transitions, and chapter transitions are server-validated.\ndiff --git a/docs/testing.md b/docs/testing.md\nindex e319854..73adcd4 100644\n--- a/docs/testing.md\n+++ b/docs/testing.md\n@@ -1,26 +1,25 @@\n # Testing\n \n ## Phase 2 coverage\n \n Unit coverage includes event reconciliation, event-to-log transformation, malformed/unknown event fallback, and event payload allowlisting. Database verification checks the five-chapter, five-location, two-route, five-artifact, and four-quest development fixture structure plus contiguous event/snapshot/audit invariants. Presets are reset with `npm run db:preset -- <name>`; default `npm run db:seed` retains the sealed acceptance start.\n \n Run the complete gate with:\n \n ```powershell\n npm run validate\n ```\n \n-The command creates a clean local runtime and disposable `validation.db`, then runs, in order:\n+The command creates a clean local runtime and disposable database, then runs Prisma generation/migration/seed, database invariants, Prettier, ESLint, TypeScript, 9 unit tests, the Chromium/WebKit browser matrix, post-acceptance database checks, production build, and two production restart proofs.\n \n-1. Prisma generation, migration deployment, idempotent seed, and baseline database invariants.\n-2. Prettier check, ESLint, and strict TypeScript.\n-3. Six Vitest state-machine/ceremony tests.\n-4. Pinned Chromium and WebKit installation.\n-5. Five cross-browser access/security/accessibility checks and one complete Chromium GM/player mutation workflow.\n-6. Post-acceptance database verification for contiguous events, matching snapshots/audits, deduplication, persistence, and undo state.\n-7. Optimized Next.js production build.\n-8. Two consecutive production start/health/stop cycles on port 3200.\n+The live workflow covers player/GM access, rate limiting, cookie isolation, prepare without disclosure, SSE release, ceremony timing/replay/preferences, persistence, duplicate rejection, award/map/solve/undo, offline pause/resume reconciliation, and heartbeat. Command Center tests add preview nonmutation, stale conflict, idempotent replay, all authenticated workspaces, mobile emergency layout, axe accessibility, and visual captures across desktop/tablet/mobile.\n \n-The mutation workflow covers invalid/correct player access, unauthenticated/wrong/correct/rate-limited GM access, cookie isolation, prepare-without-disclosure, live SSE release, 5–9 second ceremony timing, stage captures, skip/replay, mute and reduced motion, refresh persistence, duplicate rejection, artifact/map persistence, solve/undo, offline pause/resume reconciliation, heartbeat, accessibility scans, and 2560/1920/1440/430/390/landscape viewports.\n+The validated Phase 3 run reports:\n \n-Reports, traces, screenshots, and production logs are ignored and written to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\validation\\artifacts\\validation` for network-share checkouts, or `artifacts/validation` for local checkouts. A successful run currently reports 6 unit tests, 5 browser tests passing, 1 intentionally skipped cross-browser duplicate of the mutation workflow, 7 persisted events/audits, a successful build, and a successful restart proof.\n+- Unit after Phase 2 reconciliation: 16 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped.\n+- Browser: 9 passed, 0 failed, 1 intentional WebKit duplicate-workflow skip.\n+- Reconciled database: 10 contiguous events, 11 audit records, sequence 10.\n+- Build: passed; all Command Center and API routes compiled.\n+- Restart: two passes on the configured validation port.\n+\n+Artifacts are ignored and written below the validation runtime at `artifacts/validation`; Command Center screenshots are in `artifacts/validation/command-center`. `PLAYWRIGHT_BASE_URL` and `FOREVER_VALIDATION_PRODUCTION_PORT` may select isolated ports for parallel sessions.\n", "type": "input_text"}]

Completed duplicates replay their stored result. Failures retain correlation and sanitized code.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/architecture.md:5:The player reads one server-filtered `PublicSnapshot` containing chapters, released hints/annotations, visible map locations/routes, safe artifact states, visible optional mysteries, event-derived log entries, generic finale state, and per-section unseen counts. One SSE connection transports ordered sanitized events; section navigation is client-side and starts no independent polling.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/architecture.md:7:New normalized records are `MapRoute` and `ViewedContent`. Existing content models gained release, relationship, placement, and safe-label fields rather than duplicating derived display tables. `AudioPreference` now carries the cross-device preference projection; immediate UI choices may also be cached locally for offline startup.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/architecture.md:18:Server Components enforce initial access. Route handlers own authentication, validation, snapshots, and SSE. `src/server/progression.ts` is the transaction boundary; `src/domain/story.ts` owns state rules. The UI receives only a public projection, never database rows. In-memory publish accelerates same-process delivery while database replay by sequence remains authoritative after reconnect/restart.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/artifact-system.md:3:Artifacts progress through `UNKNOWN`, `SILHOUETTE`, `DISCOVERED`, `AWARDED`, `INSPECTED`, `CONNECTED`, and the future-only `ASSEMBLED` state. Unknown records expose only neutral placement structure. Silhouettes expose only an approved safe name/label. Full names, descriptions, discovery text, related chapter, and award time require a released state.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/asset-licenses.md:5:No third-party story map, game art, photographs, or ripped game audio were added. The companion shell uses CSS, text symbols, and existing procedural audio only. Any later image or sound asset must record source, author, license, modification, and in-product use here before commit.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/audit-and-event-history.md:1:# Audit and event history\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/audit-and-event-history.md:3:Progress events are the ordered player-facing/domain history; audits are administrative history. Successful commands record actor, command, time, target, event/sequence where applicable, correlation, outcome, and optional reason. Failures record a sanitized code. Credentials are never metadata.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/cinematic-system.md:3:`useCinematicTransition` is the shared cancellable sequence runner for arrival, authentication, and progression presentation. A plan has named opening, success, and failure stages. For server-backed commands the real request starts immediately alongside the opening stage. The success branch cannot begin until that request succeeds; a rejected request runs the plan's physical reversal and never reveals authoritative state. Abort on unmount prevents orphaned stage timers, duplicate starts are rejected, and CSS ambience pauses while the document is hidden.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/cinematic-system.md:5:Major full-motion durations are approximately: first arrival 7.95s, same-session re-entry 1.55s, sign-in success 4.72s, prepare 2.5s, release 4.4s in the Quartermaster plus the 7.75s player reveal, solve 2.55s, relic award 3.6s, map reveal 4.15s, pause 2.55s, resume 2.55s, and undo 2.7s. Gentle motion keeps the order with 280-420ms stages. Reduced motion keeps the narrative ordering with stages capped at 120ms.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/codex-handoff.md:9:- Commands: prepare/release/solve/complete chapter, prepare/release hint, reveal map, award artifact, discover/advance quest, release journal entry, pause/resume, undo, reconciliation\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/command-center-information-architecture.md:6:| `/quartermaster/chapters`    | Chapters      | state-machine progression              |\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/command-center-information-architecture.md:10:| `/quartermaster/quests`      | Side Quests   | discovery and advancement              |\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/command-center-information-architecture.md:15:| `/quartermaster/audit`       | Audit         | actor/outcome/correlation history      |\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/command-center-information-architecture.md:18:All routes use the authenticated shell, browser history, and server truth. Player navigation never links to them. Below 900 px the rail becomes an emergency dock with Pause/Resume, Player View, Next Hint, and Undo. `Ctrl/Cmd+K` opens the command palette, `Escape` closes overlays, and `P` opens pause/resume confirmation.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/decisions/0002-use-server-sent-events.md:5:Context: progression is server-to-player and ordered. Decision: SSE with event IDs, heartbeat, reconnect, Last-Event-ID/query recovery, and snapshot reconciliation. Alternatives: WebSockets and polling. Consequences: simpler proxy/runtime behavior; multi-instance fan-out later needs shared pub/sub.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/decisions/0003-use-event-driven-story-state.md:5:Context: refresh, duplicate delivery, close actions, replay, and undo must be safe. Decision: transactional domain state plus immutable sequenced events, snapshots, audit records, and save states. Alternatives: chapter-number mutation and client-only state. Consequences: reliable reconciliation and history at the cost of more explicit transaction code.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/game-master-controls.md:3:The Phase 3 Command Center uses select → server projection → consequence confirmation → atomic command → persistence receipt → separate delivery observation. It exposes Command Deck, Chapters, Hints, Voyage, Artifacts, Side Quests, Journal, Event Staging, Player View, Recovery, Audit, and Diagnostics.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/game-master-controls.md:5:No control sets arbitrary state. Chapter commands follow the seven-state machine, hints remain ordered, awards are unique, pause blocks progression, and undo emits a compensating event. Preview is watermarked and nonmutating. Critical finale/reset controls are absent. See [information architecture](command-center-information-architecture.md), [risk model](action-risk-model.md), and [recovery](recovery-and-reversal.md).\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/journal-system.md:3:Chapters use `LOCKED`, `TEASER`, `READY`, `REVEALING`, `ACTIVE`, `SOLVED`, and `COMPLETE`. Locked/ready chapters serialize only ordinal, state, and an explicitly safe teaser. Narrative, objective, clue, released hints, annotations, and cross-links serialize only for readable states.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/local-development.md:22:- GM username/password: `kato` / `development-captain-only`\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/parallel-development-phase-3.md:11:Existing behavior uses `src/domain/story.ts` for seven chapter states and ordered player events, `src/lib/snapshot.ts` for the player-safe projection, and `src/server/progression.ts` as the Phase 1 transaction boundary. Phase 3 needs administrative commands, staging, preview, idempotency, presence, and audit correlation. Phase 2 is likely to touch `story.ts`, both Prisma schemas, seed data, the player component, and the snapshot.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/parallel-development-phase-3.md:15:Shared files modified: `src/domain/story.ts`, `src/server/progression.ts`, `src/components/player/PlayerExperience.tsx`, both Prisma schemas, `prisma/seed.ts`, and appended Command Center CSS. New events are `HINT_PREPARED`, `MAP_REVEAL_PREPARED`, `ARTIFACT_AWARD_PREPARED`, `SIDE_QUEST_UPDATE_PREPARED`, `PLAYER_RECONCILIATION_REQUESTED`, and `NARRATIVE_MESSAGE_RELEASED`.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/phase-2-phase-3-integration.md:14:Migration order: Phase 1 init, Phase 2 additive migration, then Phase 3 command-center migration. Reconciliation tests cover older snapshots, hidden content, hint/artifact/map/quest delivery, route presence, acknowledgement, SSE reconnect, preview nonmutation, stale commands, and reversal.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/player-companion-information-architecture.md:3:The companion has six URL-addressable sections: `journal`, `chart`, `treasures`, `quests`, `log`, and `finale`. The selected section is stored in the `section` query parameter so refresh, deep links, back/forward navigation, and mobile/desktop handoff share one model. A persistent objective links back to the active journal chapter.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/player-presence-and-sync.md:16:Connected means a non-disconnected heartbeat within 45 seconds. Recently lost means evidence within 120 seconds. Older evidence is stale; no evidence is unknown. A connected transport is never labeled “viewed.” Synchronized requires an active device whose acknowledged sequence equals the campaign sequence. Future production maintenance should expire records older than 30 days.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/recovery-and-reversal.md:13:Phase 3 surfaces save points, labels only the latest as directly reversible, and preserves history through a compensating event. It never deletes the reversed event. Multi-event rollback and arbitrary restoration remain unavailable. Pause preserves released content; resume requires stale staged actions to be previewed again.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/responsive-behavior.md:5:Desktop uses the available monitor as a layered story workspace with persistent one-action navigation and compact objective access. Mobile switches to a single content column, labeled bottom navigation, safe-area padding, separate section views, and a compact return-to-clue control. The map always has a text alternative. Artifact inspection becomes a full-screen sheet on narrow screens. Fullscreen mode removes secondary chrome without removing navigation or the objective.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/security.md:5:Serialization is allowlist-based. Locked chapters omit narrative/objective/clues; unreleased hints and annotations are absent; rumored map locations omit coordinates/internal regions; unknown artifacts omit names/descriptions; hidden quests are absent and rumors expose only safe teasers; hidden finale details are never modeled in public content. SSE payloads pass through the same explicit allowlist and never stream raw stored payloads. Player routes require a campaign-bound, unexpired access identity and expose no mutation endpoint.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/security.md:9:Player access uses a bcrypt-verified campaign code and strict HTTP-only cookie. GM login uses bcrypt, database sessions, secure/strict cookies, a per-session CSRF token, generic failures, and a five-failure/15-minute fingerprint limit. Zod validates mutation input. Admin actions are audited. Logs redact passwords, access codes, cookies, and authorization headers. Player snapshots omit all chapter content until the chapter is released; tests and manual DOM/network inspection verify the gate contains no clue text.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/security.md:13:The repository was still treated as public during the automated-demo milestone. Only generic development clues, locations, messages, and credentials are committed; validation performs a deliberate diff/secret review before publication. LAN development is an explicit opt-in because binding to `0.0.0.0` exposes the disposable demo surface to peers allowed by the host firewall.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/ships-log.md:3:The Ship's Log is derived from ordered `ProgressEvent` records by `src/domain/ships-log.ts`. The transformation owns player-facing title, summary, symbol, importance, destination section, and safe target key. Unknown/internal events return no entry and never crash rendering.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/side-quest-system.md:3:Optional mysteries use `HIDDEN`, `RUMORED`, `DISCOVERED`, `ACTIVE`, `PARTIALLY_COMPLETE`, `COMPLETE`, `MISSED`, and `ARCHIVED`. Hidden records are omitted. Rumored records expose only a safe teaser. Discovered and later records may expose title, premise, ordered objectives, safe reward, and released cross-links.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/side-quest-system.md:9:  D --> A[ACTIVE]\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/side-quest-system.md:16:Side quests never block the main chapter state machine. Rewards are persisted, idempotent domain outcomes and may enrich—not gate—the future finale.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:5:Chapter transitions remain authoritative. Map, route, artifact, side-quest, and finale state changes are separate domain events projected into the same campaign snapshot. They never bypass chapter visibility. New event types include route reveal, artifact silhouette/connection, quest completion, annotation/log arrival, and finale tease/requirement changes. Undo captures every mutable companion subsystem before applying a progression action.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:13:  REVEALING --> ACTIVE\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:14:  ACTIVE --> SOLVED\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:17:  ACTIVE --> READY: undo\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:18:  SOLVED --> ACTIVE: undo\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:21:The schema stores state and ordered immutable events rather than a chapter number alone. The vertical slice releases READY directly to durable ACTIVE in one transaction so animation failure never blocks truth; REVEALING is a client ceremony phase and remains available for future server acknowledgements. Every mutation saves a restore point, increments a campaign sequence, creates an event/snapshot/audit record, then publishes after commit. Event IDs plus device ceremony acknowledgements provide idempotency.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/story-state-machine.md:23:Administrative clients cannot set state directly. They submit named commands with an expected sequence. Pause blocks progression except resume, undo, and diagnostics. Ordered hints, unique awards, side-quest transitions, and chapter transitions are server-validated.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/testing.md:15:The live workflow covers player/GM access, rate limiting, cookie isolation, prepare without disclosure, SSE release, ceremony timing/replay/preferences, persistence, duplicate rejection, award/map/solve/undo, offline pause/resume reconciliation, and heartbeat. Command Center tests add preview nonmutation, stale conflict, idempotent replay, all authenticated workspaces, mobile emergency layout, axe accessibility, and visual captures across desktop/tablet/mobile.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/theatrical-event-system.md:5:Meaningful map, artifact, quest, log, objective, and finale events reuse the ordered ceremony queue. Routine section navigation stays fast. Every event has a final-state snapshot, reduced-motion path, skip path, and stable event identity; refresh does not replay an acknowledged ceremony. See `docs/cinematic-system.md` for reusable motion primitives.\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/theatrical-event-system.md:16:  Player->>Player: Heading → story → objective → riddle → map\r\norigin/feature/game-master-command-center:docs/theatrical-event-system.md:20:`useCeremony` owns one sorted queue and AbortController; components contain no independent timeout chains. Default timing totals about 6.3 seconds. Reduced motion uses attention, grouped ink stages, map, and active state with 120ms caps. Skip aborts presentation but still reconciles the authoritative snapshot. Replay creates a local synthetic event ID and never mutates progression. Audio is initialized only by “Open the journal”; the seal tone is procedural and low-volume.\r\nor…24 tokens truncated…s-log.ts:161:  const template = copy[event.type as Never use these credentials in production.\nDATABASE_URL=\"file:./dev.db\"\nSESSION_SECRET=\"$secret\"\nGM_USERNAME=\"kato\"\nGM_PASSWORD=\"development-captain-only\"\nPLAYER_ACCESS_CODE=\"development-moonwake\"\nNEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=\"http://127.0.0.1:3000\"\nLOG_LEVEL=\"info\"\n\"@ | Set-Content -LiteralPath $environmentPath -Encoding UTF8\n        Write-Host \"Created ignored local development configuration at $environmentPath\" -ForegroundColor DarkGray\n    }\n    $required = \"DATABASE_URL\", \"SESSION_SECRET\", \"GM_USERNAME\", \"GM_PASSWORD\", \"PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE\"\n    $contents = Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $environmentPath\n    foreach ($name in $required) {\n        if ($contents -notmatch \"(?m)^$name=\") { throw \".env exists but is missing $name. ($line in Get-Content -LiteralPath $Path) {\n        if ($line -match '^\\s*#' -or $line -notmatch '=') { continue }\n        $name, $value = $line -split '=', 2\n        $value = $value.Trim().Trim('\"').Trim(\"'\")\n        [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($name.Trim(), $value, \"Process\")\n    }\n}\n\nfunction Sync-ForeverRuntime {\n    param([ValidateSet(\"development\", \"validation\")][string]$Mode = \"development\")\n    $isNetworkPath = $script:ProjectRoot.StartsWith(\"\\\\\")\n    if (-not $isNetworkPath -and $Mode -eq \"development\") { return $script:ProjectRoot }\n    $runtimeRoot = Join-Path $script:RuntimeBase $Mode\n    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $runtimeRoot)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $runtimeRoot -Force | Out-Null }\n    $resolvedBase = (Resolve-Path $script:RuntimeBase).ProviderPath\n    $resolvedRuntime = (Resolve-Path $runtimeRoot).ProviderPath\n    if (-not $resolvedRuntime.StartsWith($resolvedBase, [StringComparison]::OrdinalIgnoreCase)) { throw \"Unsafe runtime mirror path.\" }\n    & robocopy $script:ProjectRoot $runtimeRoot /MIR /XD .git .forever node_modules node_modules.failed .next artifacts coverage test-results playwright-report /XF *.db *.db-journal *.log .forever-dev.json .forever-lock.sha | Out-Null\n    if ($LASTEXITCODE -gt 7) { \"node_modules/next/dist/bin/next\", \"start\", \"-H\", \"127.0.0.1\", \"-p\", \"$Port\" -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -WindowStyle Hidden -RedirectStandardOutput $stdout -RedirectStandardError $stderr -PassThru\n    try { Wait-ForeverHttp -Url \"http://127.0.0.1:$Port\" -Seconds 45 }\n    finally { if (-not $process.HasExited) { Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force; [void]$process.WaitForExit(10000) } }\n}\n\nWrite-Host \"`n==> Proving production restart safety\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\nTest-ProductionStart -Port $productionPort\nTest-ProductionStart -Port $productionPort\nWrite-Host \"`nFull validation passed. Reports and screenshots: $env:VALIDATION_ARTIFACTS\" -ForegroundColor Green\n\n===== scripts/verify-database.ts =====\r\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\n\nconst db = new PrismaClient();\nconst acceptance = process.argv.includes(\"--acceptance\");\nconst preset = process.argv.includes(\"--preset\");\n\nfunction assert(condition: unknown, message: string): asserts condition {\n  if (!condition) throw new Error(`Database verification failed: ${message}`);\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const campaign = await db.campaign.findUnique({\n    where: { slug: \"development-forever-treasure\" },\n    include: {\n      chapters: true,\n      artifacts: true,\n      mapLocations: true,\n      mapRoutes: true,\n      sideQuests: true,\n      events: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } },\n      snapshots: true,\n      awards: true,\n      auditLogs: true,\n      saveStates: true,\n      playerAccesses: true,\n    },\n  });\n  assert(campaign, \"the development campaign is missing\");\n  assert(campaign.chapters.length >= 5, \"the complete development chapter set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.artifacts.length >= 5, \"the complete development artifact set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.mapLocations.length >= 5, \"the complete development map set is required\");\n  assert(campaign.mapRoutes.length >= 2, \"development route segments are required\");\n  assert(campaign.sideQuests.length >= 4, \"development side-quest states are required\");\n  assert(campaign.playerAccesses.length === 1, \"exactly one development player access is required\");\n  assert(campaign.currentSequence === campaign.events.length, \"campaign sequence must equal its ordered event count\");\n  assert(\n    campaign.events.every((event, index) => event.sequence === index + 1),\n    \"event sequences must be contiguous\",\n  );\n  assert(\n    new Set(campaign.events.map((event) => event.sequence)).size === campaign.events.length,\n    \"event sequences must be unique\",\n  );\n  assert(campaign.snapshots.length === campaign.events.length, \"every progression event must have a campaign snapshot\");\n  assert(\n    campaign.auditLogs.filter((entry) => entry.outcome === \"SUCCEEDED\" && entry.action !== \"DEVELOPMENT_SEED\").length >=\n      campaign.events.length,\n    \"every progression event must have a successful audit record\",\n  );\n  assert(campaign.awards.length <= campaign.artifacts.length, \"artifact awards must remain unique\");\n\n  if (!acceptance && !preset) {\n    assert(campaign.currentSequence === 0, \"a fresh validation database must start at sequence zero\");\n    assert(campaign.chapters[0].state === \"LOCKED\", \"a fresh chapter must be locked\");\n  } else if (acceptance) {\n    const eventTypes = new Set(campaign.events.map((event) => event.type));\n    for (const expected of [\n      \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\",\n      \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\n      \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\",\n      \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\n      \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\n      \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\",\n      \"STATE_REVERTED\",\n    ]) {\n      assert(eventTypes.has(expected), `acceptance run did not record ${expected}`);\n    }\n    assert(campaign.awards.length >= 1, \"acceptance run must persist an artifact award\");\n    assert(\n      campaign.mapLocations.some((location) => location.revealedAt),\n      \"acceptance run must persist a released map location\",\n    );\n    assert(campaign.saveStates.length > 0, \"acceptance run must retain undo save states\");\n  }\n\n  console.log(\n    `Database verified: ${campaign.events.length} events, ${campaign.auditLogs.length} audit entries, sequence ${campaign.currentSequence}.`,\n  );\n}\n\nmain().finally(() => \"nearly-complete-shell\",\n  \"all-empty\",\n  \"long-log\",\n  \"mobile-stress-test\",\n]);\n\nconst statesByPreset: Record<string, string[]> = {\n  \"awaiting-first-release\": [\"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"artifact-award-ready\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"TEASER\", \"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"side-quest-active\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"READY\", \"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"mid-voyage\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"TEASER\", \"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"nearly-complete-shell\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"READY\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"long-log\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"TEASER\", \"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n  \"mobile-stress-test\": [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\", \"TEASER\", \"LOCKED\", \"LOCKED\"],\n};\n\nasync function main() {\n  if (!supportedPresets.has(preset)) throw new Error(`Unknown development preset: ${preset}`);\n  const gmUsername = process.env.GM_USERNAME ?? \"kato\";\n  const gmPassword = process.env.GM_PASSWORD ?? practice page is complete.\",\n    ],\n    [\n      \"The Cartographer’s Long Development Heading for Narrow Screens\",\n      \"A deliberately long development narrative tests reading rhythm without
