// Move the mouse to a point by the provided x and y coordinates.\n  scroll(options: ScrollOptions): Promise<void>; // Scroll by a delta from a specific viewport coordinate.\n  type(options: TypeOptions): Promise<void>; // Type text at the current focus.\n}\n\ninterface DomCUAAPI {\n  click(options: DomClickOptions): Promise<void>; // Click a DOM node by its id from the visible DOM snapshot.\n  double_click(options: DomClickOptions): Promise<void>; // Double-click a DOM node by its id.\n  get_visible_dom(): Promise<unknown>; // Return a filtered DOM with node ids for interactable elements.\n  keypress(options: DomKeypressOptions): Promise<void>; // Press control characters at the currently focused element (focus it first via click/dblclick).\n  scroll(options: DomScrollOptions): Promise<void>; // Scroll either the page or a specific node (if node_id provided) by deltas.\n  type(options: DomTypeOptions): Promise<void>; const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({code:"var browserDocsFull = await browser.documentation();\nvar browserDocsLines = browserDocsFull.split(\"\\n\");\nnodeRepl.write(browserDocsLines.slice(245, 345).join(\"\\n\"));",title:"Finish browser setup",timeout_ms:30000});
for (const c of (r?.content ?? [])) { if (c.type === "text") text(c.text); else if (c.type === "image") image(c); }

Switch to the most stable visible attribute from the snapshot or use a scoped DOM-based click path.\r\n\r\n## Fallback Guidance\r\n\r\n- Prefer stable `href` values copied from the snapshot over guessed URL patterns.\r\n- Prefer scoped attribute selectors over global text selectors.\r\n- Use `getByText(...)` only when role-based or attribute-based locators are not reliable, and scope it to a container whenever possible.\r\n- Prefer attributes copied directly from the latest snapshot over inferred semantics, fragile CSS chains, or positional selectors.\r\n- Do not invent likely selectors. If the snapshot does not clearly expose a unique target, fetch a fresh snapshot and reassess before acting.\n\n\n# Additional Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when you need one of these topics:\n- `confirmations`: read before asking the user for browser confirmation\n- `browser-troubleshooting`: read when a selected browser fails while interacting with a page\n- `file-uploads`: read before uploading files through a webpage\n- `screenshots`: read when the user asks for screenshots\n\n# Additional Capabilities\n## Browser Capabilities\n- `visibility`: Use to show or hide the browser to the user, and to determine the browser's current visibility. Keep browser work in the background unless the user asks to see it or live viewing is useful. When the browser should be visible, call set(true).\n  Read with `await (await browser.capabilities.get(\"visibility\")).documentation()`.\n- `viewport`: Controls an explicit browser viewport override for responsive or device-size testing. Use it when a task calls for specific dimensions or breakpoint validation; otherwise leave it unset so the browser uses its normal viewport. Reset temporary overrides before finishing unless the user asked to keep them.\n  Read with `await (await browser.capabilities.get(\"viewport\")).documentation()`.\n## Tab Capabilities\n- `pageAssets`: List assets already observed in the current page state and bundle selected assets into a temporary local artifact.\n  Read with `await (await tab.capabilities.get(\"pageAssets\")).documentation()`.\n\n# API Reference\n\nUse this as the supported `agent.browsers.*` surface.\n\n```ts\n// Installed by setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis }).\n// browser was selected during bootstrap.\ninterface Agent {\n  browsers: Browsers; // API for finding and selecting browsers.\n  documentation: Documentation; // API for reading packaged browser-use documentation by name.\n}\n\ninterface Browsers {\n  get(id: string): Promise<Browser>; // Get a browser by id or client type.", "type": "input_text"}]

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-17T04:25:00.614Z

Live inspection confirms the polished desktop player and Game Master surfaces render, both mobile layouts avoid horizontal page overflow, the first-arrival ceremony has staged sea/open-book states, and the refresh fix preserves an opened player journal. It also confirms a real defect the current tests miss: the Side Quests screen offers “Advance quest” for `RUMORED` and `HIDDEN` quests, and its server preview approves the transition even though the command implementation can skip directly to completion. I’m doing one controlled local persistence check next, using only the disposable development campaign.

(typeof chapterStates)[number];\r\n   5: \r\n   6: export const eventTypes = [\r\n   7:   \"CAMPAIGN_STARTED\",\r\n   8:   \"CAMPAIGN_PAUSED\",\r\n   9:   \"CAMPAIGN_RESUMED\",\r\n  10:   \"CHAPTER_PREPARED\",\r\n  11:   \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\",\r\n  12:   \"CHAPTER_REVEAL_STARTED\",\r\n  13:   \"CHAPTER_REVEAL_COMPLETED\",\r\n  14:   \"CHAPTER_SOLVED\",\r\n  15:   \"HINT_RELEASED\",\r\n  16:   \"HINT_PREPARED\",\r\n  17:   \"MAP_REVEAL_PREPARED\",\r\n  18:   \"ARTIFACT_AWARD_PREPARED\",\r\n  19:   \"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATE_PREPARED\",\r\n  20:   \"ARTIFACT_AWARDED\",\r\n  21:   \"SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\",\r\n  22:   \"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\",\r\n  23:   \"JOURNAL_ENTRY_ADDED\",\r\n  24:   \"MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED\",\r\n  25:   \"MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED\",\r\n  26:   \"ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED\",\r\n  27:   \"ARTIFACT_CONNECTED\",\r\n  28:   \"SIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\",\r\n  29:   \"JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED\",\r\n  30:   \"PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED\",\r\n  31:   \"FINALE_TEASED\",\r\n  32:   \"FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED\",\r\n  33:   \"GM_MESSAGE_RELEASED\",\r\n  34:   \"FINALE_UNLOCKED\",\r\n  35:   \"STATE_REVERTED\",\r\n  36:   \"PLAYER_RECONCILIATION_REQUESTED\",\r\n  37:   \"NARRATIVE_MESSAGE_RELEASED\",\r\n  38: ] as const;\r\n  39: export type ProgressEventType = (typeof eventTypes)[number];\r\n  40: \r\n  41: export const validTransitions: Record<ChapterState, readonly ChapterState[]> = {\r\n  42:   LOCKED: [\"TEASER\", \"READY\"],\r\n  43:   TEASER: [\"READY\", \"LOCKED\"],\r\n  44:   READY: [\"REVEALING\", \"LOCKED\"],\r\n  45:   REVEALING: [\"ACTIVE\", \"READY\"],\r\n  46:   ACTIVE: [\"SOLVED\", \"READY\"],\r\n  47:   SOLVED: [\"COMPLETE\", \"ACTIVE\"],\r\n  48:   COMPLETE: [\"SOLVED\"],\r\n  49: };\r\n  50: \r\n  51: export function canTransition(from: ChapterState, to: ChapterState) {\r\n  52:   return validTransitions[from].includes(to);\r\n  53: }\r\n  54: \r\n  55: export const gmActionSchema = z.object({\r\n  56:   action: z.enum([\r\n  57:     \"PREPARE_CHAPTER\",\r\n  58:     \"RELEASE_CHAPTER\",\r\n  59:     \"MARK_SOLVED\",\r\n  60:     \"AWARD_ARTIFACT\",\r\n  61:     \"REVEAL_MAP\",\r\n  62:     \"REVEAL_ROUTE\",\r\n  63:     \"REVEAL_ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE\",\r\n  64:     \"CONNECT_ARTIFACTS\",\r\n  65:     \"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\",\r\n  66:     \"UPDATE_SIDE_QUEST\",\r\n  67:     \"COMPLETE_SIDE_QUEST\",\r\n  68:     \"ADD_JOURNAL_ANNOTATION\",\r\n  69:     \"ADD_LOG_ENTRY\",\r\n  70:     \"TEASE_FINALE\",\r\n  71:     \"UPDATE_FINALE_REQUIREMENT\",\r\n  72:     \"UNDO_LAST\",\r\n  73:     \"PAUSE\",\r\n  74:     \"RESUME\",\r\n  75:   ]),\r\n  76:   campaignSlug: z.string().min(3).max(80),\r\n  77:   confirmation: z.literal(true),\r\n  78:   targetKey: z.string().min(1).max(120).optional(),\r\n  79:   value: z.string().max(1000).optional(),\r\n  80: });\r\n  81: \r\n  82: export type PublicChapter = {\r\n  83:   ordinal: number;\r\n  84:   state: ChapterState;\r\n  85:   title?: string;\r\n  86:   narrative?: string;\r\n  87:   objective?: string;\r\n  88:   riddle?: string;\r\n  89:   teaser?: string;\r\n  90:   hints?: Array<{ ordinal: number; body: string; releasedAt: string; unseen: boolean }>;\r\n  91:   annotations?: Array<{ key: string; title: string; body: string; createdAt: string; unseen: boolean }>;\r\n  92:   related?: { mapKey?: string; artifactKey?: string; sideQuestKey?: string };\r\n  93:   unseen?: boolean;\r\n  94: };\r\n  95: \r\n  96: export type PublicMapLocation = {\r\n  97:   key: string;\r\n  98:   state: string;\r\n  99:   label: string;\r\n 100:   name: string;\r\n 101:   regionLabel?: string;\r\n 102:   locationType?: string;\r\n 103:   description?: string;\r\n 104:   exactness?: string;\r\n 105:   x?: number;\r\n 106:   y?: number;\r\n 107:   mobileX?: number;\r\n 108:   mobileY?: number;\r\n 109:   chapterOrdinal?: number;\r\n 110:   sideQuestKey?: string;\r\n 111:   unseen: boolean;\r\n 112: };\r\n 113: \r\n 114: export type PublicArtifact = {\r\n 115:   key: string;\r\n 116:   state: string;\r\n 117:   name?: string;\r\n 118:   safeName?: string;\r\n 119:   category?: string;\r\n 120:   description?: string;\r\n 121:   discoveryText?: string;\r\n 122:   silhouetteLabel?: string;\r\n 123:   displayX: number;\r\n 124:   displayY: number;\r\n 125:   assemblyGroup?: string;\r\n 126:   assemblyPosition?: string;\r\n 127:   connectedArtifactKey?: string;\r\n 128:   chapterOrdinal?: number;\r\n 129:   awardedAt?: string;\r\n 130:   unseen: boolean;\r\n 131: };\r\n 132: \r\n 133: export type PublicSideQuest = {\r\n 134:   key: string;\r\n 135:   state: string;\r\n 136:   title?: string;\r\n 137:   teaser?: string;\r\n 138:   description?: string;\r\n 139:   objectives?: Array<{ ordinal: number; body: string; complete: boolean }>;\r\n 140:   reward?: { type: string; label?: string };\r\n 141:   completionSummary?: string;\r\n 142:   chapterOrdinal?: number;\r\n 143:   mapLocationKey?: string;\r\n 144:   artifactKey?: string;\r\n 145:   unseen: boolean;\r\n 146: };\r\n 147: \r\n 148: export type PublicLogEntry = {\r\n 149:   key: string;\r\n 150:   sequence: number;\r\n 151:   title: string;\r\n 152:   summary: string;\r\n 153:   timestamp: string;\r\n 154:   symbol: string;\r\n 155:   importance: \"quiet\" | \"notable\" | \"major\";\r\n 156:   section: \"journal\" | \"chart\" | \"treasures\" | \"quests\" | \"log\" | \"finale\";\r\n 157:   targetKey?: string;\r\n 158:   unseen: boolean;\r\n 159: };\r\n 160: \r\n 161: export type PublicSnapshot = {\r\n 162:   campaign: { slug: string; title: string; status: string };\r\n 163:   sequence: number;\r\n 164:   chapter: PublicChapter;\r\n 165:   chapters: PublicChapter[];\r\n 166:   artifacts: PublicArtifact[];\r\n 167:   mapLocations: PublicMapLocation[];\r\n 168:   mapRoutes: Array<{\r\n 169:     key: string;\r\n 170:     fromKey: string;\r\n 171:     toKey: string;\r\n 172:     ordinal: number;\r\n 173:     state: string;\r\n 174:     annotation?: string;\r\n 175:     unseen: boolean;\r\n 176:   }>;\r\n 177:   sideQuests: PublicSideQuest[];\r\n 178:   sideQuest: { title: string; state: string } | null;\r\n 179:   log: PublicLogEntry[];\r\n 180:   finale: {\r\n 181:     state: string;\r\n 182:     teaser?: string;\r\n 183:     requirements: Array<{ key: string; label: string; current: number; target: number; optional?: boolean }>;\r\n 184:     unseen: boolean;\r\n 185:   };\r\n 186:   unseen: Record<\"journal\" | \"chart\" | \"treasures\" | \"quests\" | \"log\" | \"finale\", number>;\r\n 187: };\r\n 188: \r\n 189: export type ClientProgressEvent = {\r\n 190:   id: string;\r\n 191:   type: ProgressEventType;\r\n 192:   sequence: number;\r\n 193:   payload: Record<string, unknown>;\r\n 194:   releaseAt: string;\r\n 195: };\r\n 196: \r\n 197: export function mergeEvents<T extends { id: string; sequence: number }>(existing: T[], incoming: T[]) {\r\n 198:   const byId = new Map(existing.map((event) => [event.id, event]));\r\n 199:   for (const event of incoming) byId.set(event.id, event);\r\n 200:   return [...byId.values()].sort((a, b) => a.sequence - b.sequence);\r\n 201: }\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "--- 3 ---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.2 \"title\", \"rewardLabel\"],\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "--- 4 ---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n 501: function Workspace({\r\n 502:   name,\r\n 503:   status,\r\n 504:   choose,\r\n 505:   stage,\r\n 506:   refresh,\r\n 507: }: {\r\n 508:   name: string;\r\n 509:   status: Status;\r\n 510:   choose: (action: Action) => Promise<void>;\r\n 511:   stage: (action: Action) => Promise<void>;\r\n 512:   refresh: () => Promise<void>;\r\n 513: }) {\r\n 514:   if (name === \"chapters\") return <Chapters status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 515:   if (name === \"hints\") return <Hints status={status} choose={choose} stage={stage} />;\r\n 516:   if (name === \"voyage\") return <Voyage status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 517:   if (name === \"artifacts\") return <Artifacts status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 518:   if (name === \"quests\") return <Quests status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 519:   if (name === \"journal\") return <Journal status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 520:   if (name === \"events\") return <Events status={status} />;\r\n 521:   if (name === \"player-view\") return <PlayerView status={status} />;\r\n 522:   if (name === \"recovery\") return <Recovery status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 523:   if (name === \"audit\") return <Audit status={status} />;\r\n 524:   if (name === \"diagnostics\") return <Diagnostics status={status} refresh={refresh} choose={choose} />;\r\n 525:   return <Deck status={status} choose={choose} />;\r\n 526: }\r\n 527: \r\n 528: function Deck({ status, choose }: { status: Status; choose: (action: Action) => Promise<void> }) {\r\n 529:   const last = status.events[0];\r\n 530:   const deckActions = quickActions.map((action) =>\r\n 531:     action.command === \"PAUSE\" && status.campaign.status === \"PAUSED\"\r\n 532:       ? {\r\n 533:           command: \"RESUME\" as const,\r\n 534:           label: \"Resume Campaign\",\r\n 535:           consequence: \"Revalidate staged actions and return the voyage to active status.\",\r\n 536:         }\r\n 537:       : action,\r\n 538:   );\r\n 539:   return (\r\n 540:     <>\r\n 541:       <section className=\"command-hero\">\r\n 542:         <div>\r\n 543:           <p className=\"eyebrow\">Active operation</p>\r\n 544:           <h2>\r\n 545:             Chapter {status.chapter.ordinal}: {status.chapter.title}\r\n 546:           </h2>\r\n 547:           <p>{status.chapters.find((item) => item.ordinal === status.chapter.ordinal)?.objective}</p>\r\n 548:         </div>\r\n 549:         <span className={`state-flag state-${status.chapter.state.toLowerCase()}`}>{status.chapter.state}</span>\r\n 550:       </section>\r\n 551:       <div className=\"deck-grid\">\r\n 552:         <section className=\"instrument-panel immediate\">\r\n 553:           <PanelTitle title=\"Immediate operations\" detail=\"Fast, deliberate commands\" />\r\n 554:           <div className=\"command-buttons\">\r\n 555:             {deckActions.map((action) => (\r\n 556:               <button\r\n 557:                 key={action.command}\r\n 558:                 aria-label={action.label}\r\n 559:                 onClick={() => void choose(action)}\r\n 560:                 disabled={action.command === \"UNDO_LAST\" && !status.recovery.some((item) => item.reversible)}\r\n 561:               >\r\n 562:                 <b>{action.label}</b>\r\n 563:                 <span>{action.consequence}</span>\r\n 564:               </button>\r\n 565:             ))}\r\n 566:           </div>\r\n 567:         </section>\r\n 568:         <section className=\"instrument-panel player-signal\">\r\n 569:           <PanelTitle title=\"Player signal\" detail=\"Evidence, not assumption\" />\r\n 570:           <div className=\"signal-orb\" data-state={status.presence.state} />\r\n 571:           <h3>{title(status.presence.state)}</h3>\r\n 572:           <dl>\r\n 573:             <Row label=\"Last heartbeat\" value={formatAge(status.presence.lastSeenAt)} />\r\n 574:             <Row label=\"Active devices\" value={String(status.presence.activeDevices)} />\r\n 575:             <Row label=\"Last acknowledged\" value={`Sequence ${status.presence.acknowledgedSequence}`} />\r\n 576:             <Row label=\"Current route\" value={status.presence.route ?? \"Unknown\"} />\r\n 577:           </dl>\r\n 578:           <Link href=\"/quartermaster/player-view\">Inspect sanitized Player View â†’</Link>\r\n 579:         </section>\r\n 580:         <section className=\"instrument-panel upcoming\">\r\n 581:           <PanelTitle title=\"Upcoming dispatches\" detail={`${status.stagedActions.length} persisted`} />\r\n 582:           {status.stagedActions.length ? (\r\n 583:             <ol className=\"ledger-list\">\r\n 584:               {status.stagedActions.slice(0, 5).map((item) => (\r\n 585:                 <li key={item.id}>\r\n 586:                   <span className=\"status-token amber\">{item.status}</span>\r\n 587:                   <b>{title(item.command)}</b>\r\n 588:                   <small>\r\n 589:                     {item.expectedSequence === status.campaign.sequence ? \"Validated\" : \"Needs revalidation\"}\r\n 590:                   </small>\r\n 591:                 </li>\r\n 592:               ))}\r\n 593:             </ol>\r\n 594:           ) : (\r\n 595:             <Empty>No staged releases. The player cannot see this ledger.</Empty>\r\n 596:           )}\r\n 597:         </section>\r\n 598:         <section className=\"instrument-panel recent\">\r\n 599:           <PanelTitle title=\"Recent truth\" detail={last ? `Last action sequence ${last.sequence}` : \"No events\"} />\r\n 600:           <ol className=\"event-stream\">\r\n 601:             {status.events.slice(0, 7).map((event) => (\r\n 602:               <li key={event.id}>\r\n 603:                 <span>{event.sequence}</span>\r\n 604:                 <div>\r\n 605:                   <b>{title(event.type)}</b>\r\n 606:                   <small>\r\n 607:                     {event.actor} Â· {new Date(event.createdAt).toLocaleTimeString()}\r\n 608:                   </small>\r\n 609:                 </div>\r\n 610:               </li>\r\n 611:             ))}\r\n 612:           </ol>\r\n 613:         </section>\r\n 614:       </div>\r\n 615:     </>\r\n 616:   );\r\n 617: }\r\n 618: \r\n 619: function Chapters({ status, choose }: { status: Status; choose: (action: Action) => Promise<void> }) {\r\n 620:   return (\r\n 621:     <section className=\"instrument-panel full\">\r\n 622:       <PanelTitle title=\"Chapter operations\" detail=\"State-machine controlled; no arbitrary mutation\" />\r\n 623:       <div className=\"chapter-ledger\">\r\n 624:         {status.chapters.map((chapter) => (\r\n 625:           <article key={chapter.id}>\r\n 626:             <div className=\"chapter-number\">{String(chapter.ordinal).padStart(2, \"0\")}</div>\r\n 627:             <div>\r\n 628:               <span className={`status-token state-${chapter.state.toLowerCase()}`}>{chapter.state}</span>\r\n 629:               <h2>{chapter.title}</h2>\r\n 630:               <p>{chapter.objective}</p>\r\n 631:               <small>\r\n 632:                 {chapter.hints.length} hints Â·{\" \"}\r\n 633:                 {chapter.revealedAt ? `released ${new Date(chapter.revealedAt).toLocaleString()}` : \"not released\"}\r\n 634:               </small>\r\n 635:             </div>\r\n 636:             <div className=\"row-actions\">\r\n 637:               {chapter.state === \"LOCKED\" && <button onClick={() => void choose(quickActions[0])}>Prepare</button>}\r\n 638:               {chapter.state === \"READY\" && (\r\n 639:                 <button onClick={() => void choose(quickActions[1])}>Preview & release</button>\r\n 640:               )}\r\n 641:               {chapter.state === \"ACTIVE\" && <button onClick={() => void choose(quickActions[3])}>Mark solved</button>}\r\n 642:               {chapter.state === \"SOLVED\" && (\r\n 643:                 <button\r\n 644:                   onClick={() =>\r\n 645:                     void choose({\r\n 646:                       command: \"COMPLETE_CHAPTER\",\r\n 647:                       label: \"Complete Chapter\",\r\n 648:                       consequence: \"Close the solved chapter and make its completion durable.\",\r\n 649:                     })\r\n 650:                   }\r\n 651:                 >\r\n 652:                   Complete\r\n 653:                 </button>\r\n 654:               )}\r\n 655:               {chapter.state === \"COMPLETE\" && <span>Complete â€” no forward action</span>}\r\n 656:             </div>\r\n 657:           </article>\r\n 658:         ))}\r\n 659:       </div>\r\n 660:     </section>\r\n 661:   );\r\n 662: }\r\n 663: \r\n 664: function Hints({\r\n 665:   status,\r\n 666:   choose,\r\n 667:   stage,\r\n 668: }: {\r\n 669:   status: Status;\r\n 670:   choose: (action: Action) => Promise<void>;\r\n 671:   stage: (action: Action) => Promise<void>;\r\n 672: }) {\r\n 673:   return (\r\n 674:     <section className=\"instrument-panel full\">\r\n 675:       <PanelTitle title=\"Hint control center\" detail=\"Ordered release with quick next-hint control\" />\r\n 676:       <button className=\"primary-command\" onClick={() => void choose(quickActions[2])}>\r\n 677:         Release next eligible hint\r\n 678:       </button>\r\n 679:       {status.chapters.map((chapter) => (\r\n 680:         <div className=\"hint-group\" key={chapter.id}>\r\n 681:           <h2>\r\n 682:             Chapter {chapter.ordinal} Â· {chapter.title}\r\n 683:           </h2>\r\n 684:           {chapter.hints.length ? (\r\n 685:             chapter.hints.map((hint) => {\r\n 686:               const action: Action = {\r\n 687:                 command: \"RELEASE_HINT\",\r\n 688:                 targetKey: hint.id,\r\n 689:                 label: `Release Hint ${hint.ordinal}`,\r\n 690:                 consequence: `Expose only Hint ${hint.ordinal} to the player.`,\r\n 691:               };\r\n 692:               return (\r\n 693:                 <article key={hint.id}>\r\n 694:                   <span className={`status-token ${hint.releasedAt ? \"live\" : \"sealed\"}`}>\r\n 695:                     {hint.releasedAt ? \"RELEASED\" : \"SEALED\"}\r\n 696:                   </span>\r\n 697:                   <b>Hint {hint.ordinal}</b>\r\n 698:                   <p>{hint.body}</p>\r\n 699: To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n  39: \r\n  40: ### Portable manual setup\r\n  41: \r\n  42: ```bash\r\n  43: git clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\n  44: cd treasurehuntSoT\r\n  45: npm ci\r\n  46: cp .env.example .env\r\n  47: npm run db:generate\r\n  48: npm run db:migrate\r\n  49: npm run db:seed\r\n  50: npm run dev\r\n  51: ```\r\n  52: \r\n  53: Set unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n  54: \r\n  55: ## Commands\r\n  56: \r\n  57: | Command                           | Purpose                                                                |\r\n  58: | --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n  59: | `npm run dev:full`                | Idempotent setup, migration, seed, health check, and background server |\r\n  60: | `npm run dev:stop`                | Safely stop only the recorded development process                      |\r\n  61: | `npm run validate`                | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof          |\r\n  62: | `npm run dev`                     | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                        |\r\n  63: | `npm run build`                   | Production build                                                       |\r\n  64: | `npm start`                       | Start production build                                                 |\r\n  65: | `npm test`                        | Complete Phase 2/3 domain, visibility, presence, and ceremony tests    |\r\n  66: | `npm run test:e2e`                | Cross-browser player and Command Center acceptance matrix              |\r\n  67: | `npm run db:migrate`              | Apply local SQLite migrations                                          |\r\n  68: | `npm run db:seed`                 | Load replaceable development content                                   |\r\n  69: | `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage` | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset                |\r\n  70: | `npm run db:generate:mysql`       | Generate production MySQL client                                       |\r\n  71: \r\n  72: Full setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md).\r\n  73: \r\n  74: ## Repository workflow\r\n  75: \r\n  76: `main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n  77: \r\n  78: ## Important limitations\r\n  79: \r\n  80: The full story, final art/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural; no external copyrighted assets are present. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "--- 2 ---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n   1: # Local development\r\n   2: \r\n   3: ## One-command Windows startup\r\n   4: \r\n   5: Double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd` or run `npm run dev:full`. The idempotent launcher:\r\n   6: \r\n   7: 1. Finds Node/npm and explains what is missing when neither a normal installation nor the bundled Codex runtime is available.\r\n   8: 2. Creates `.env` only when absent; existing local configuration is preserved and validated.\r\n   9: 3. Mirrors network-share checkouts to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development` while keeping dependencies, databases, logs, and generated artifacts out of Git.\r\n  10: 4. Runs `npm ci` only when `package-lock.json` changed or dependencies are missing.\r\n  11: 5. Generates Prisma, applies the versioned SQLite migration, and runs the idempotent development seed.\r\n  12: 6. Refuses to take an occupied port, starts the server as a hidden recorded process, and waits for an HTTP health response.\r\n  13: 7. Prints the player and GM URLs, disposable credentials, and stop command.\r\n  14: \r\n  15: Run `npm run dev:stop` before switching branches or replacing the runtime. Shutdown verifies the recorded PID is the expected Next.js development process before stopping it.\r\n  16: \r\n  17: ## URLs and credentials\r\n  18: \r\n  19: - Player URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n  20: - Invitation phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n  21: - GM URL: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n  22: - GM username/password: `kato` / `development-captain-only`\r\n  23: \r\n  24: The launcher-generated `.env` is ignored. Change these values for any shared environment. Never reuse them for deployment.\r\n  25: \r\n  26: ## LAN opt-in\r\n  27: \r\n  28: `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan` binds Next.js to `0.0.0.0` and prints the best detected IPv4 URLs. LAN mode is intentionally not the one-click default because it exposes a development server and disposable credentials to the local network. If another device cannot connect, allow the selected TCP port through Windows Firewall and confirm that corporate Wi-Fi/client-isolation policy permits peer traffic.\r\n  29: \r\n  30: ## Portable fallback\r\n  31: \r\n  32: On macOS/Linux or when automation is unavailable:\r\n  33: \r\n  34: ```bash\r\n  35: npm ci\r\n  36: cp .env.example .env\r\n  37: npm run db:generate\r\n  38: npm run db:migrate\r\n  39: npm run db:seed\r\n  40: npm run dev\r\n  41: ```\r\n  42: \r\n  43: Use Node 24 and npm 11.9.0; `.nvmrc`, `.node-version`, `packageManager`, and `package-lock.json` define the reproducible toolchain.\r\n  44: \r\n  45: ## Clean-clone continuation\r\n  46: \r\n  47: An authorized session on another computer needs only Git, Node/npm, and this repository. Clone `main`, copy `.env.example` or use the Windows launcher, then run `npm run validate`. No database, generated Prisma client, Playwright browser state, artifact, or machine path is required from the originating computer.\r\n  48: \r\n  49: ## Troubleshooting\r\n  50: \r\n  51: - **Port 3000 is occupied:** stop that process or run `scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Port 3001`. The launcher never kills an unknown listener.\r\n  52: - **Existing `.env` is incomplete:** the launcher preserves it and identifies each missing key. Add the key; it will not overwrite local secrets.\r\n  53: - **Startup fails:** inspect `%LOCALAPPDATA%\\ForeverTreasureCompanion\\development\\.forever\\logs`.\r\n  54: - **Database is disposable:** stop the app, remove the ignored local `prisma/dev.db`, and rerun startup. For validation, `npm run validate` always rebuilds `validation.db` from migrations.\r\n  55: - **Browser binaries are missing:** `npm run validate` installs pinned Chromium and WebKit builds automatically.\r\n  56: - **UNC/network checkout is slow:** the first mirror/install is expected to take longer; subsequent runs retain the dependency cache and only synchronize project files.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "--- 3 ---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n   1: # Deployment\r\n   2: \r\n   3: Target: Debian, Node 24, MySQL 8, NGINX, HTTPS, and systemd. Do not deploy from development seed credentials.\r\n   4: \r\n   5: 1. Create MySQL database/user and set `DATABASE_URL=mysql://...`.\r\n   6: 2. For a new database run `npm ci`, `npm run db:generate:mysql`, `npm run db:migrate:mysql:init`, then `npm run build`. Record future MySQL migrations under `prisma/mysql-migrations` before production use.\r\n   7: 3. Install `deploy/forever-treasure.service`, place secrets in `/etc/forever-treasure.env`, and proxy with `deploy/nginx.conf`.\r\n   8: 4. Send application logs to journald; retain NGINX access/error logs under `/var/log/nginx`.\r\n   9: \r\n  10: Back up with daily encrypted `mysqldump --single-transaction`, retain seven daily/four weekly copies off-host, and test quarterly restore into a separate database. Restore by stopping the service, importing a verified dump, applying pending migrations, and starting/reconciling. Generate secrets with `openssl rand -base64 48`.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "--- 4 ---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n   1: # Known limitations\r\n   2: \r\n   3: ## Phase 2 boundaries\r\n   4: \r\n   5: - The repository is public; final story and surprise content is intentionally blocked.\r\n   6: - Finale unlock/assembly is structural only and cannot enter `READY` or later states through Phase 2 controls.\r\n   7: - SSE fan-out remains process-local and needs Redis or equivalent before multi-instance production deployment.\r\n   8: - Initial Ship's Log projection is capped at 250 entries; cursor pagination is future scale work.\r\n   9: - Production MySQL migration must be exercised against an actual MySQL 8 integration environment.\r\n  10: - Final art/audio, real content authoring, and production deployment belong to later phases.\r\n  11: \r\n  12: - SSE fan-out is process-local; database replay is durable, but multi-instance production needs Redis/pub-sub.\r\n  13: - The Phase 3 preview is a sanitized state projection rather than a pixel-identical embedded Player Companion ceremony timeline; explicit replay targeting is not implemented.\r\n  14: - The full mutation workflow runs once in Chromium to avoid cross-project database contention; privacy, route protection, accessibility, and mobile behavior also run in WebKit.\r\n  15: - Windows LAN access is opt-in and may still require a firewall rule or network-policy change; localhost is the safe default.\r\n  16: - SQLite and MySQL schemas are intentionally parallel; schema edits must update both and add SQL to the connector-specific migration histories until a generation workflow is added.\r\n  17: - Final story content, private media, polished licensed audio, production monitoring, and deployment remain out of scope.\r\n  18: - Scheduling stores a future timestamp and stale-state evidence but has no background execution worker; scheduled actions require Game Master review and release.\r\n  19: - Recovery exposes only the latest safe single-step reversal. Full dependency-graph multi-event rollback is intentionally unavailable.\r\n  20: - Presence is durable per device but cleanup is documented rather than automated; production should expire records older than 30 days.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

!== \"chromium\",\n+    \"The shared-database mutation workflow runs once to avoid cross-project contention.\",\n+  );\n+  await page.goto(\"/quartermaster\");\n+  await page.getByLabel(\"Captain’s name\").fill(process.env.GM_USERNAME!);\n+  await page.getByLabel(\"Passphrase\").fill(process.env.GM_PASSWORD!);\n+  await page.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Enter the chart room\" }).click();\n+  await expect(page.getByRole(\"heading\", { name: \"The Forever Treasure\" })).toBeVisible();\n+\n+  let status = await (await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\")).json();\n+  const hiddenQuest = status.sideQuests.find((item: { state: string }) => item.state === \"HIDDEN\");\n+  expect(hiddenQuest).toBeTruthy();\n+  const invalidPreview = await page.request.post(\"/api/gm/preview\", {\n+    data: {\n+      command: \"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\",\n+      campaignSlug: status.campaign.slug,\n+      expectedSequence: status.campaign.sequence,\n+      targetKey: hiddenQuest.key,\n+      payload: {},\n+      preview: true,\n+    },\n+  });\n+  expect(invalidPreview.ok()).toBeTruthy();\n+  expect(await invalidPreview.json()).toMatchObject({\n+    canExecute: false,\n+    prerequisites: [\"Discover the side quest before advancing it.\"],\n+  });\n+\n+  async function command(command: string, targetKey?: string) {\n+    status = await (await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\")).json();\n+    const response = await page.request.post(\"/api/gm/commands\", {\n+      headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": status.csrfToken },\n+      data: {\n+        command,\n+        campaignSlug: status.campaign.slug,\n+        expectedSequence: status.campaign.sequence,\n+        idempotencyKey: crypto.randomUUID(),\n+        targetKey,\n+        payload: {},\n+        confirmation: true,\n+      },\n+    });\n+    const body = await response.json();\n+    expect(response.ok(), JSON.stringify(body)).toBeTruthy();\n+    return body;\n+  }\n+\n+  const discovered = await command(\"DISCOVER_SIDE_QUEST\", hiddenQuest.key);\n+  expect(discovered.event).toMatchObject({\n+    type: \"SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED\",\n+    payload: { key: hiddenQuest.key },\n+  });\n+  expect((await command(\"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\", hiddenQuest.key)).event.type).toBe(\"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\");\n+  expect((await command(\"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\", hiddenQuest.key)).event).toMatchObject({\n+    type: \"SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED\",\n+    payload: { key: hiddenQuest.key, objectiveOrdinal: 1 },\n+  });\n+  expect((await command(\"ADVANCE_SIDE_QUEST\", hiddenQuest.key)).event).toMatchObject({\n+    type: \"SIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED\",\n+    payload: { key: hiddenQuest.key },\n+  });\n+\n+  status = await (await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\")).json();\n+  const mapTarget = status.mapLocations.find((item: { revealedAt: string | null }) => !item.revealedAt);\n+  expect(mapTarget).toBeTruthy();\n+  const mapResult = await command(\"REVEAL_MAP\", mapTarget.key);\n+  expect(mapResult.event.payload.key).toBe(mapTarget.key);\n+  status = await (await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\")).json();\n+  expect(\n+    status.audit.some(\n+      (entry: { action: string; correlationId: string | null }) =>\n+        entry.action === \"REVEAL_MAP\" && entry.correlationId === mapResult.correlationId,\n+    ),\n+  ).toBe(true);\n+\n+  const artifactTarget = status.artifacts.find((item: { awarded: boolean }) => !item.awarded);\n+  expect(artifactTarget).toBeTruthy();\n+  const artifactResult = await command(\"AWARD_ARTIFACT\", artifactTarget.key);\n+  expect(artifactResult.event.payload.key).toBe(artifactTarget.key);\n+\n+  status = await (await page.request.get(\"/api/gm/status\")).json();\n+
